California Home Visiting Program
California Home Visiting Program
California Home Visiting Program (CHVP)
Lake County’s
Home Visit Program is an evidenced-based program designed by
Healthy Families America
to support pregnant and parenting families in our community.
Specially trained nurses and family advocates called Community Health Workers (CHWs) help pregnant people, new parents and their families get the health and support services they need. The program promotes healthy pregnancies, nurturing parenting skills, positive child development, and strengthen overall positive outcomes for families and communities.
Services are voluntary and provided in the comfort of a family’s home or location of their choosing.
What services does a Home Visitor do?
What services does a Home Visitor do?
Visiting Nurses will check maternal and baby health
Child development checks
Talk with you about family health and support needs
Help you use the services that you want
Provide you with information about:
Child health, growth and development
Maternal physical and mental health
Social support programs
Economic support programs
Healthcare services
Breastfeeding
Parent and child bonding
Who is eligible?
Who is eligible?
Pregnant or postpartum parents with low incomes
Families with children aged 0-3 months with low incomes
Pregnant and parenting families experiencing any of the following:
Inadequate income
Substance use
Depression or other mental health challenge
Domestic violence
Children with special health care needs (physical, developmental and emotional health) aged 0-21 years.
What services can a Home Visitor help with?
What services can a Home Visitor help with?
Medical Care
Dental Care
Mental Health Care
Child growth and development
Parent and infant bonding
Home safety
Family safety
Family planning
Any other services you and your home visitor think will help your baby and family
For more information or to sign up, please contact
Kitty Wertz, Public Health Nurse
707-263-1090
Lake County’s Home Visiting Program is support by funds from the California Department of Public Health, Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health Division
